purpose: Socle JSON pédagogique pour une application La Voie du dedans consacrée à Ibn ʿAṭāʾ Allāh al-Iskandarī : méditations par thèmes, carnet de bord quotidien, parcours de lecture et intégration future des textes arabes/français.
editorial_position: Ce fichier est volontairement pédagogique et applicatif. Il ne remplace pas une édition critique. Il prépare le terrain pour intégrer ensuite les textes arabes vocalisés, traductions françaises originales et commentaires vérifiés.

source_works.status: JSON v2 structuré, 300 entrées, sans arabe vocalisé ni traduction française finale

source_works.title_fr: L’illumination dans l’abandon du faux gouvernement de soi
source_works.status: EPUB arabe extractible ; utilisé ici par thèmes et synthèse, sans reproduction longue

daily_engine.principle: Le carnet doit éviter de répéter chaque jour le même mot. Il doit proposer plusieurs portes selon l’état, le contexte et le moment de la journée.

morning_flow.label: Où ma journée va se jouer ?
Ce que je pose pour aujourd’hui
Guidance proposée
morning_flow.rule: croiser état + contexte + intentions pour proposer 1 à 3 modules, jamais toujours le même

day_flow.label: Une phrase courte issue du module choisi, sans long scrolling

evening_flow.label: Ce que j’ai honoré / ce que je dois reprendre
Finir par une parole adressée à Dieu

meditation_collections.title: Ouvrir la journée
meditation_collections.use: Choix d’une ou plusieurs vigilances du jour, sans obligation d’écriture longue.

meditation_collections.title: Traverser une tension relationnelle
meditation_collections.use: Quand l’utilisateur s’est énervé, a blessé, s’est senti rejeté ou doit réparer.

meditation_collections.title: Travail, argent et subsistance
meditation_collections.use: Pour anxiété matérielle, pression professionnelle, comparaison.

meditation_collections.title: Relire le soir sans se juger
meditation_collections.use: Bilan du soir : reconnaître, réparer, remercier, remettre.

meditation_collections.title: Voir la nafs et se transformer
meditation_collections.use: Parcours d’accompagnement intérieur sur plusieurs semaines.

meditation_collections.title: Fatigue, fermeture, vulnérabilité
meditation_collections.use: Quand l’utilisateur arrive lourd, fermé, épuisé ou sans envie d’écrire.

tanwir: axe central de l’ouvrage al-Tanwīr fī Isqāṭ al-Tadbīr
développement sur le fait de ne pas rivaliser avec les décrets divins
lataif: utile en arrière-plan shādhilī : relation au maître, acceptation des dévoilements et des épreuves

thematic_modules.teaching: For Ibn ʿAṭāʾ Allāh, laying down tadbīr does not mean becoming passive. It means stripping the ego of the illusion that it governs reality. The human being acts, works, cares, decides, but ceases to believe that his anxiety, his tension or his inner control can manufacture the decree. This teaching frees an inner space: do what depends on you, then hand the outcome back to God.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme speaks directly to anguish, to over-control, to the need to foresee every reaction, every scenario, every safeguard. It helps distinguish responsibility from agitation. Responsibility clarifies action; agitation feeds fear.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: The heart learns to move from “I must hold everything together” to “I respond to what is entrusted to me”. The practice consists in acting without taking oneself for the source of the outcome.

matin: Name one thing I genuinely have to do.
Name one thing I cannot control.
Choose a simple action, then hand the result back to God.
journee: When worry returns, breathe and ask: is this an action to take or an outcome to hand over?
Return to the body, slow the decision, do not mistake inner urgency for divine command.
soir: Did I act rightly without wanting to possess the result?
Where did I confuse trust with abandoning my responsibilities?

pitfalls: Using letting-go to avoid a responsibility.
Believing that trust dispenses with effort.
Judging oneself harshly when anxiety returns.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which situation today asked me to act without possessing the outcome?
thematic_modules.app_use: Module central pour le carnet de bord : matin pour poser l’intention, soir pour distinguer action juste et contrôle anxieux.

tanwir: confiance liée au rizq et à l’abandon du tadbīr
lataif: voie shādhilī : confiance transmise par les maîtres

thematic_modules.teaching: Tawakkul is not a soothing idea added on top of fear; it is a shift in the centre of gravity. One ceases to lean on one's works, one's intelligence or one's foresight as though they were sovereign. One uses them, but does not worship them. God becomes the support, and the means remain mere means.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: A lack of trust often shows itself in panic before delay, obsession with the answer, a loss of inner dignity when nothing comes. Trust restores a vaster sense of time.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Receive the delay as a place where the heart is educated: God does not always answer according to my script, but He never ceases to lead.

matin: Choose a difficulty and say: I take the means, I do not own the end.
journee: Replace one rumination with a sober action or a short invocation.
soir: Where did I wait differently? Where did I try to force?

pitfalls: Confusing trust with naivety.
Refusing concrete causes.
Using God to guarantee one's own script.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: In which area do I need to learn an active trust rather than a tense waiting?
thematic_modules.app_use: À proposer quand l’utilisateur choisit “inquiétude”, “attente”, “retard”, “besoin de sécurité”.

thematic_modules.title_fr: The right of the present moment

tanwir: distinction avant le décret, pendant le décret, après le décret
lataif: enseignements de la voie sur les états et les stations

thematic_modules.teaching: The present moment holds a spiritual right. This right is not merely “enjoying the present”, but recognising what God asks now: patience, gratitude, repentance, a word withheld, service, rest, prayer, silence. The past cannot be replaced; the future cannot be possessed; the present is the place of response.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme helps those scattered between regret and anticipation. It recentres attention on a possible micro-obedience.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Ask: what is the right worship of this instant? It may be to act, to wait, to mend, to keep silent, to ask forgiveness, to set a limit.

matin: Identify the likely spiritual duty of the day.
journee: A twenty-second pause: what is the right of this moment?
soir: Did I honour at least one moment, instead of living the whole day inside my head?

pitfalls: Turning the present into a spiritual performance.
Leaping towards tomorrow to avoid what is asked now.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which moment of my day called me to a truer response?
thematic_modules.app_use: Très utile pour le carnet : créer un sélecteur “droit du moment” sans obliger l’utilisateur à écrire.
thematic_modules.title_en: The right of the present moment

tanwir: long développement sur le rizq, la faim du cœur, la recherche des moyens et l’inquiétude
lataif: vie des maîtres : sobriété, confiance, service

thematic_modules.teaching: Ibn ʿAṭāʾ Allāh treats rizq as a place where the heart is tried. Sustenance is not merely an economic matter; it reveals what the soul believes it must possess in order to feel secure. The path does not forbid work. It purifies fear, comparison, haste and greed.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: The fear of lack can become an identity. It breeds anticipation, hardness in relationships, exhaustion, jealousy, or the need to prove one's worth.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Work without worshipping work, receive without clinging, ask without inwardly debasing oneself before creatures.

matin: Choose a sober and dignified way of seeking rizq.
journee: When financial fear rises, return to a concrete action, then to a request to God.
soir: Did I seek my sustenance with dignity? Did I let fear govern my character?

pitfalls: Spiritualising a material negligence.
Making work into an idol.
Confusing inner poverty with a sought-after precariousness.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Today, did my relationship to rizq draw me closer to God or harden me within?
thematic_modules.app_use: À relier aux contextes travail, famille, argent, avenir, fatigue, comparaison sociale.

thematic_modules.title_fr: Sincerity and the purification of intention

tanwir: agir sans rivaliser avec le décret
lataif: adab de la voie, transmission et service

thematic_modules.teaching: Sincerity is not merely “having a good intention”. It consists in not making the act a property of the ego. The act needs a living heart, otherwise it remains a motionless form. Ikhlāṣ renders action transparent: it is no longer a means of magnifying oneself, but a response to God.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme illuminates the need to be seen, validated, admired or reassured by the deed accomplished. It does not condemn the human need for recognition; it teaches one not to be its prisoner.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Before the action: why? During: for whom? After: can I refrain from appropriating what was given?

matin: Choose a discreet action without seeking any return.
journee: When I want to be acknowledged, recognise the need without granting it power.
soir: Did I do something to be seen? Was I able to offer an action without reclaiming it?

quick_choices: I was hurt by the absence of return
pitfalls: Turning sincerity into an obsession with purity.
Judging oneself for any longing for recognition.
Denying legitimate relational needs.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which action can I render more transparent before God?
thematic_modules.app_use: À utiliser pour les bilans du soir, les actions de service, la parole publique, le travail.
thematic_modules.title_en: Sincerity and the purification of intention

tanwir: faire sa part sans posséder la récompense
lataif: zuhd, hauteur d’aspiration, rectitude

thematic_modules.teaching: The problem is not the work, but the reliance upon the work. A good deed may raise or imprison, according to what the heart makes of it. If it produces pride, hardness or comparison, it needs to be washed. If it produces gratitude and humility, it becomes a place of nearness.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme addresses demanding, scrupulous, perfectionist profiles, or those who grow discouraged the moment they fall.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Act in earnest, then attribute the capacity to act to God. Do not despair after a failing; do not grow proud after a success.

matin: Carry out a simple work without aiming at an image of oneself.
journee: After a success, say inwardly: this was granted to me.
soir: What did my action produce in my heart: humility or entitlement?

pitfalls: Abandoning works on the pretext of not relying on them.
Measuring one's spiritual worth by output.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Today, did I use a good deed to draw nearer, or to prove myself?
thematic_modules.app_use: Très bon module pour relier intention du matin et bilan du soir.

thematic_modules.title_fr: Patience before the decrees and the vexations

tanwir: dix raisons qui soutiennent le serviteur devant les décrets
temps avant/pendant/après le décret
lataif: épreuves et états des maîtres

thematic_modules.teaching: Patience is not gritting one's teeth in silence. It consists in receiving the trial without losing adab with God, with oneself and with others. In al-Tanwīr, the decrees are understood as places where God educates the heart, opens understanding and reveals the hidden supports.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: Vexation reveals the nervous system, the attachments and the expectations. It shows where the ego demands that reality obey its script.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Slow down before reacting. Seek what is asked: to endure, to speak, to mend, to protect oneself, to ask for help, to hand over.

matin: Choose a concrete patience: traffic, a child, work, waiting, pain, criticism.
journee: Before a reaction, breathe three times and name: I am vexed, but I can keep adab.
soir: Which vexation taught me my attachment?

pitfalls: Confusing patience with passivity before injustice.
Repressing anger instead of educating it.
Believing oneself spiritual because one says nothing.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: In which vexation was I able to remain a servant rather than an owner of reality?
thematic_modules.app_use: Module majeur pour l’utilisateur quotidien : famille, travail, fatigue, imprévu.
thematic_modules.title_en: Patience before the decrees and the vexations

tanwir: besoin et bienfaits comme occasions de gratitude
lataif: reconnaissance envers les maîtres et la transmission

thematic_modules.teaching: Gratitude sees the gift without stopping at the surface of the gift. It thanks creatures according to adab, but it never loses sight of the Source. It transforms the everyday: food, help, a word, rest, an occasion to learn all become signs.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: Gratitude works upon the habit of lack, the automatic complaint, comparison, and blindness to the supports already present.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Learn to thank God without becoming ungrateful towards people, and to thank people without forgetting God.

matin: Name three supports already present.
journee: Thank one person concretely, without flattery.
soir: Which blessing did I pass through without recognising it?

pitfalls: Using gratitude to deny a real suffering.
Saying thank you without changing one's attention.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which ordinary blessing can become a sign again today?
thematic_modules.app_use: À proposer dans les bilans positifs, réussites, apaisements, repas, relation.

tanwir: retour du cœur à Dieu après la prétention de gestion
lataif: adab des maîtres et rectification intérieure

thematic_modules.teaching: Repentance is not a scene of self-hatred. It is the living movement by which the heart returns to God, recognises what drew it away, and seeks reparation where this is necessary. The failing can become a place of humility if it does not turn into despair.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This module is essential for those who judge themselves harshly after anger, a wounding word, a negligence or a relapse.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Recognise without dramatising, ask forgiveness without theatrics, mend without destroying oneself.

matin: Plan a possible reparation if a relationship remains damaged.
journee: When a failing occurs: stop, recognition, return, reparation.
soir: What do I need to lay down before God? To whom must I make some reparation?

pitfalls: Confusing repentance with toxic shame.
Repenting inwardly but avoiding outward reparation.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which failing can become, today, a door to humility and return?
thematic_modules.app_use: Très utile pour les exemples concrets : colère avec un enfant, parole dure, jugement, négligence.

tanwir: ne pas perdre l’apaisement dans les décrets
lataif: états intérieurs et stations

thematic_modules.teaching: Hope without seriousness becomes frivolity; fear without mercy becomes closure. Ibn ʿAṭāʾ Allāh brings the heart back to a fear that awakens and a hope that sustains. The two guard against two maladies: despair and complacency.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: Some punish themselves inwardly; others avoid looking at what is wrong. The pairing of hope and fear allows a lucidity that is not violent.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Look at one's failing without losing sight of God; look at mercy without losing adab.

matin: Choose a vigilance without harshness.
journee: When I fall, return to hope; when I grow proud, return to fear.
soir: Did I lean towards complacency or towards despair?

pitfalls: Using fear to guide.
Using hope to make light of the wounds one has caused.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: On which side does my heart need to be rebalanced today?
thematic_modules.app_use: À proposer après un bilan difficile ou une réussite trop appropriée.

tanwir: ne pas s’approprier les états
lataif: stations, états, dévoilements

thematic_modules.teaching: Contraction and expansion are two educations of the heart. When the heart tightens, it learns humility, need, patience. When it opens, it learns gratitude and non-appropriation. No state should become an identity.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme helps one not to panic when spiritual momentum wanes, and not to believe one has arrived when all seems luminous.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: In contraction: do not conclude that God abandons. In expansion: do not conclude that the ego possesses the light.

matin: Name my state without reducing myself to it.
journee: If closed: gentleness and constancy. If open: gratitude and sobriety.
soir: How did I inhabit my state without identifying with it?

pitfalls: Seeking the pleasant state rather than God.
Reading every psychological state as a certain spiritual sign.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Did my inner state serve to bring me back, or to tell myself a story?
thematic_modules.app_use: À relier à “comment je viens aujourd’hui”, mais de manière cohérente : état du matin, état de fin de journée.

tanwir: le besoin comme voie de munājāt et de retour
lataif: spiritualité shādhilie : pauvreté noble et dépendance de Dieu

thematic_modules.teaching: The need for God is not a shameful weakness; it is the deep truth of the servant. The soul suffers when it must appear self-sufficient. The path teaches one to transform need into orientation, not into humiliation before creatures.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme touches loneliness, exhaustion, the sense of not coping, the shame of asking for help.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Recognise: I need God, and I can ask for help without losing my dignity.

matin: Name a real need without shame.
journee: Ask God for help before hardening.
soir: Did I hide my need behind pride or complaint?

pitfalls: Seeking emotional dependency and calling it the need for God.
Refusing concrete help.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which need can become, today, a prayer rather than a shame?
thematic_modules.app_use: À proposer lors de fatigue, tristesse, surcharge, solitude, demande de soutien.

tanwir: ne pas faire du monde le centre de gestion intérieure
lataif: zuhd des maîtres, hauteur d’aspiration

thematic_modules.teaching: The world is not despised as creation; it becomes dangerous when it takes the place of the Lord in the heart. Zuhd is a sobriety of relation: to use without worshipping, to receive without losing oneself, to leave inwardly whatever demands our servanthood.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme illuminates attachments to images, possessions, statuses, seductions, screens, social outcomes.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Ask not: do I have things? but: what is it that possesses me?

matin: Choose a concrete sobriety.
journee: Observe a craving without obeying it at once.
soir: What possessed me today?

pitfalls: Confusing zuhd with contempt for the world.
Judging those who possess.
Turning sobriety into a spiritual image.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which attachment asks to be seen with honesty?
thematic_modules.app_use: Pour thématiques consommation, comparaison, téléphone, statut, achats, ambition.

thematic_modules.title_fr: The soul, the passions and inner education

tanwir: tadbīr du moi, choix propre, passions déguisées
lataif: stations, discipline, adab des maîtres

thematic_modules.teaching: The nafs is not merely an external enemy to be crushed; it is the zone of the ego that takes itself for the centre. It can disguise itself as spirituality, as prudence, as exactingness, as woundedness, as the need for control. The education consists in recognising its movements without handing them the direction.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This module speaks of the automatisms: reaction, justification, anger, the need to be right, victimhood, hidden pride.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: See the nafs without hating it. Take it in hand without violence. Bring it back to servanthood.

matin: Identify an automatism to watch.
journee: When I justify myself quickly, take a pause.
soir: Which movement of the nafs did I see? How did I accompany it?

pitfalls: Detesting one's personality.
Calling nafs every legitimate need.
Using spirituality to dissociate from oneself.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which inner movement did I see today without obeying it entirely?
thematic_modules.app_use: Très important pour les bilans quotidiens et les situations relationnelles.
thematic_modules.title_en: The soul, the passions and inner education

tanwir: ne pas parler depuis la rivalité intérieure avec le décret
lataif: adab du maître, parole subtile, conseils, invocations

thematic_modules.teaching: Speech reveals the state of the heart. To speak gently does not mean to speak softly or to avoid every limit. It means choosing a word that does not serve the nafs: not wounding to relieve oneself, not advising to dominate, not falling silent to punish.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme answers to daily life: family, child, marriage, work, messages, social networks. Speech is an immediate place of purification.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Before speaking: is it true? necessary? right in its form? at the right moment? from which inner state?

matin: Choose a vigilance of speech: not to judge, not to interrupt, not to harden.
journee: If there is tension: slow the sentence, lower the intensity, keep the truth.
soir: Which word drew near? Which word wounded? What reparation?

pitfalls: Confusing gentleness with self-effacement.
Using truth as a weapon.
Falling silent out of contempt.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which word of today deserves to be entrusted to God?
thematic_modules.app_use: À mettre dans les intentions du matin et dans le bilan du soir ; prévoir choix multiples sans écriture obligatoire.

thematic_modules.title_fr: Anger, irritation and the return to presence

tanwir: contrariété comme lieu de décret et de compréhension
lataif: patience, hauteur d’aspiration, adab avec les maîtres et les gens

thematic_modules.teaching: Anger often shows a wounded pretension: reality, the other, or the child did not obey what I wanted. The path does not ask one to deny the emotion, but to take back the direction before the nafs speaks in our place.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This module is made for ordinary scenes: tiredness, a child, a spouse, a colleague, a delay, noise, a felt injustice.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: The first victory is not never to feel anger; it is to create a space between the impulse and the word.

matin: Foresee a situation where I am liable to grow irritated.
journee: A bodily stop: release the jaw, the shoulders, the breath; then, and only then, speak.
soir: Did I make reparation after losing my temper? Did I understand what was touched in me?

pitfalls: Accusing oneself instead of making reparation.
Justifying anger by the truth of the situation.
Confusing apparent calm with resentment.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: In which scene could I have placed a breath between myself and my reaction?
thematic_modules.app_use: À intégrer comme thème concret, car beaucoup d’utilisateurs viennent au carnet après une scène de colère.
thematic_modules.title_en: Anger, irritation and the return to presence

tanwir: sortir de la revendication du moi
lataif: vertus des maîtres, science et humilité

thematic_modules.teaching: True humility does not contemplate itself. The moment the ego says “I am humble”, it takes back a place. Ibn ʿAṭāʾ Allāh links humility to the contemplation of the divine grandeur: it is not to lower oneself artificially, it is to see more truly.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme helps distinguish humility from a lack of self-esteem. Humility frees; shame crushes.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Do not seek to appear small. Seek to see God greater, and the ego finds its measure again.

matin: Choose not to put oneself at the centre in a given situation.
journee: Receive a remark without defending oneself at once.
soir: Where did I want the last word? Where did I accept to learn?

pitfalls: Calling a self-deprecation humility.
Using modesty in order to be admired.
Refusing every limit out of spiritual pride.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: What helped me today to take back my rightful place?
thematic_modules.app_use: À relier aux situations de critique, réussite, comparaison, autorité.

tanwir: compréhension du décret comme ouverture intérieure
lataif: versets, hadiths, paroles des maîtres expliqués selon les gens de la voie

thematic_modules.teaching: Beneficial knowledge is not the accumulation of information. It illuminates the heart, draws it out of its wrappings, increases noble fear, modesty before God and rightness towards people. A knowledge that makes one hard, superior or cold must be questioned anew.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme speaks to readers, researchers, teachers, those who love to understand. It recalls that understanding must transform presence, not merely enlarge discourse.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Read a wisdom, then ask: what does this change in my way of being today?

matin: Choose a notion to live rather than to possess.
journee: Turn a piece of knowledge into a concrete adab.
soir: Did what I learned make me nearer, humbler, gentler?

pitfalls: Collecting texts without transformation.
Using knowledge in order to judge.
Believing that spiritual emotion is worth understanding.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which piece of knowledge can become, today, a conduct?
thematic_modules.app_use: Très important pour pages de lecture/méditation par thème.

tanwir: service, prière, demande et abandon
lataif: hizb d’Abū al-ʿAbbās, hizb d’Abū al-Ḥasan, invocations

thematic_modules.teaching: The wird is a faithfulness more than a performance. Ibn ʿAṭāʾ Allāh distinguishes the inner momentum from the regular obligation: even when the heart feels nothing, constancy keeps a door open. Dhikr is not merely repetition; it becomes presence if the heart consents.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme helps those who begin and then give up, or who judge their practice the moment it is not intense.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Small, regular, sincere. A humble faithfulness is better than a spectacular enthusiasm with no morrow.

matin: Choose a dhikr that is short and sustainable.
journee: Tie a remembrance to a daily gesture: walking, waiting, a transition.
soir: Did I keep a small faithfulness without judging myself?

pitfalls: Turning the dhikr into a counter of pride.
Giving up because one feels nothing.
Confusing regularity with rigidity.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which small faithfulness can support my day without weighing it down?
thematic_modules.app_use: Permettre des pratiques légères, non culpabilisantes, avec suivi hebdomadaire.

tanwir: ordonner la prière aux siens, servir plutôt que s’agiter sur le rizq
lataif: hizb, invocations, adab de la voie

thematic_modules.teaching: The path does not replace the obligations with inner states. It seeks to render the obligations alive. Prayer, family service, concrete responsibility and daily adab are places of nearness, not obstacles to a dreamed-of spirituality.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme helps those who seek a spirituality elsewhere than in their actual day. It reconciles practice and ordinary life.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Do not flee reality on the pretext of depth. God educates within the duties close at hand.

matin: Identify an obligation to honour with presence.
journee: Do an ordinary task with the intention of service.
soir: Did I disdain a simple duty while seeking something nobler?

pitfalls: Using meditation to avoid prayer or responsibilities.
Stiffening within the obligation without heart.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which close-at-hand duty is, today, my most real path?
thematic_modules.app_use: À relier au quotidien : maison, travail, enfants, prière, engagement.

thematic_modules.title_fr: Companionship, master and the Shādhilī heritage

tanwir: adressé au frère sur le chemin, pédagogie de l’accompagnement
lataif: ouvrage entier sur Abū al-ʿAbbās al-Mursī et Abū al-Ḥasan al-Shādhilī

thematic_modules.teaching: The spirituality of Ibn ʿAṭāʾ Allāh is set within a transmission. The master is not a historical ornament: he represents a living pedagogy, a correction of the ego, an adab, an orientation. In application, this can become a “Shādhilī heritage” space: understanding the masters without superficial imitation.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme helps to avoid narcissistic self-guidance: no one sees himself entirely alone.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Seek presences, readings and companionships that make one truer, humbler, more responsible.

matin: Receive a word of wisdom as orientation, not as ornament.
journee: Observe who nourishes my heart and who agitates it.
soir: Which influence did I let in today?

pitfalls: Idealising the master.
Replacing God with fascination for a figure.
Refusing every correction.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which inner or outer company drew me closer to the truth?
thematic_modules.app_use: À utiliser pour pages biographiques, lignage, citations, parcours de lecture.
thematic_modules.title_en: Companionship, master and the Shādhilī heritage

thematic_modules.title_fr: Light of the heart and unveiling

tanwir: les lumières soutiennent le serviteur devant les décrets
lataif: dévoilements, réalités, stations

thematic_modules.teaching: Light is not a spectacle. It is that by which the heart sees more truly. It may come and then depart if the heart remains cluttered. The point is not to pursue experiences, but to empty out what prevents reception: agitation, attachments, hardness, pretension.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme helps distinguish inner clarity from mental excitement. True light simplifies, calms, makes one responsible.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Ask: what clutters my heart today? Which figure of the world fills the whole mirror?

matin: Choose one clutter to lay down.
journee: When the heart closes, lighten one thing: a word, a screen, a judgement, haste.
soir: Which moment brought a little clarity? What veiled it?

pitfalls: Seeking visions.
Confusing emotional intensity with light.
Believing oneself special because one has understood something.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: What darkened or lit up my heart today?
thematic_modules.app_use: À relier à méditation, lecture, silence, surcharge mentale.
thematic_modules.title_en: Light of the heart and unveiling

tanwir: compréhension de Dieu dans le décret
lataif: dévoilements et réalités expliqués par al-Mursī

thematic_modules.teaching: Contemplation is not a withdrawal from the world; it is learning to see the world without the world veiling God. The heart travels by thought, but thought must become a lamp, not a labyrinth. Presence transforms the way we look upon ourselves, upon people, upon causes, upon trials.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme speaks to very mental people: thinking can illumine or imprison. True contemplation makes one more available to the real.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Move from analysis to presence: what is God showing me in this situation, without my reducing everything to my own story?

matin: Choose a contemplative gaze: see a sign within the ordinary.
journee: Pause: look without commenting for a few moments.
soir: Was my thinking a lamp or an agitation?

pitfalls: Escaping into abstraction.
Calling a noble rumination contemplation.
Neglecting concrete actions.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which thought gave me light, and which thought drew me away?
thematic_modules.app_use: À proposer pour lectures profondes, méditation thématique, fin de journée.

thematic_modules.title_fr: People as trial and as mirror

tanwir: ne pas laisser les réactions des créatures gouverner le cœur
lataif: compagnonnage, adab, reconnaissance de la voie

thematic_modules.teaching: People can be support, mirror, trial or veil. Ibn ʿAṭāʾ Allāh does not ask us to flee everyone; he asks us not to depend inwardly on people's welcome, praise, presence or rejection. The relationship becomes a place of purification.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme touches the wound of not being acknowledged, the fear of being rejected, excessive expectation, anger before ingratitude.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Receive people without making them the source of absolute rest.

matin: Choose one relationship to live with less expectation.
journee: If I am ignored or misunderstood, return to God before reacting.
soir: Whom have I burdened with a role only God can carry?

pitfalls: Using detachment to become cold.
Denying the human need for relationship.
Spiritualising everything instead of setting a limit.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which relationship showed me today my need for recognition or rest?
thematic_modules.app_use: Essentiel pour les contextes famille, couple, travail, amitié.
thematic_modules.title_en: People as trial and as mirror

tanwir: sortir de la gestion de son image
lataif: honneur véritable des saints et non recherche de réputation

thematic_modules.teaching: Praise can intoxicate and criticism can purify, but the reverse is also possible according to the state of the heart. The stake is not to let the outer image define the inner reality. What people see of us is neither wholly false nor wholly true: it must be handed back to God.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme works upon sensitivity to others' regard, dependence on validation, shame and pride.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: When I am praised: gratitude and caution. When I am criticised: listening and discernment without collapse.

matin: Do not seek to control an image.
journee: Receive feedback without swelling up nor destroying myself.
soir: Which outer regard took up too much space?

pitfalls: Pretending to be indifferent.
Despising all criticism.
Believing all praise.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Today, did my image govern my heart?
thematic_modules.app_use: À proposer après interactions sociales, réseaux, travail, conflits.

tanwir: ne pas aimer son propre choix plus que le choix divin
lataif: amour des maîtres, voie shādhilie, héritage spirituel

thematic_modules.teaching: We often become servants of what we love without lucidity. True love frees and orients; idolised attachment enslaves, troubles and makes possessive. The path does not kill human love; it returns it to its source and to its adab.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme sheds light on emotional dependence, jealousy, fear of loss, the need to please, fusion.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Love without making the other an absolute. Receive relationships as gifts, not as inner divinities.

matin: Observe an attachment without judging it.
journee: Within a relationship, choose one act of non-possessive love.
soir: What do I love to the point of losing my inner freedom?

pitfalls: Confusing detachment with coldness.
Feeling guilty for loving.
Spiritualising an unexamined dependence.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which love asks to be purified of possession?
thematic_modules.app_use: À relier aux relations, famille, couple, objets, projets, image de soi.

tanwir: ne pas chercher les signes au lieu de l’adab
lataif: chapitre sur les karāmāt, puis vie d’Abū al-ʿAbbās et d’Abū al-Ḥasan

thematic_modules.teaching: The karāmāt are not the central criterion of sainthood. Ibn ʿAṭāʾ Allāh refocuses upon uprightness, adab, the knowledge of God and the transformation of the heart. Signs may sustain, but they become a snare if the soul seeks them in order to feel special.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme guards against fascination, the pursuit of experiences, the confusion between intensity and maturity.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Ask for fewer spectacular signs and more truth in one's character.

matin: Choose uprightness rather than the exceptional.
journee: Observe the desire to be special.
soir: Did I seek an experience or a real transformation?

pitfalls: Taking the rare for the true.
Despising authentic signs.
Building a spiritual identity on the extraordinary.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which simple transformation is worth more than a spectacular sign?
thematic_modules.app_use: À utiliser pour encadrer toute page sur les saints, miracles et dévoilements.

thematic_modules.title_fr: Family life, work and the inner path

tanwir: responsabilité, prière des proches, rizq, besoin, service
lataif: adab vivant transmis dans la voie

thematic_modules.teaching: The ordinary day is not set apart from the path. Child, spouse, colleague, tiredness, money, meals, speech, lateness: these are the places where the soul reveals its pretensions and where the heart can return. The journal must therefore be concrete, not only contemplative.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: This theme prevents an abstract spirituality. The user must acknowledge his real day, with its tensions, its responsibilities and its limits.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: Turn an ordinary scene into a place of adab: respond differently, repair, give thanks, set a limit, ask forgiveness.

matin: Choose a concrete situation in which to live the path.
journee: A micro-pause before a transition: home, work, message, meal.
soir: In which ordinary scene did I meet my real state?

pitfalls: Separating meditation from conduct.
Seeking an unreachable ideal for a real day.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: Which ordinary scene was today my true place of practice?
thematic_modules.app_use: Module transversal indispensable pour rendre le site utilisable tous les jours.
thematic_modules.title_en: Family life, work and the inner path

thematic_modules.title_fr: Intimate prayers and speech addressed to God

tanwir: besoin, rizq, décret et retour en prière
lataif: invocations et hizb des maîtres dans les derniers chapitres

thematic_modules.teaching: The munājāt transforms knowledge into speech addressed. One no longer speaks merely of God; one speaks to God. The intimate prayers of Ibn ʿAṭāʾ Allāh give a language to need, to poverty, to gratitude, to helplessness, to shame and to hope.
thematic_modules.psychological_reading: For many, writing is difficult. The munājāt can offer short inner formulations that help one set things down without analysing for hours.
thematic_modules.spiritual_direction: When the mind keeps turning, pass to address: “O God, here is my state.”

matin: Choose a short inner request.
journee: Turn a tension into a silent address.
soir: End with a sentence addressed to God rather than a self-judgement.

pitfalls: Reciting without presence.
Using intimate prayer to avoid a concrete repair.
thematic_modules.journal_prompt: If I were to address a single sentence to God this evening, which would it be?
thematic_modules.app_use: Créer un mode “prière intime” avec phrases courtes, adaptées au thème du jour.
thematic_modules.title_en: Intimate prayers and speech addressed to God

for_next_ai: Ne pas publier les traductions anglaises modernes telles quelles sans vérification des droits.
Ajouter le texte arabe vocalisé du Kitāb al-Ḥikam depuis une source arabe fiable.
Traduire en français de manière originale et homogène, en conservant un style méditatif clair.
Pour chaque sagesse, produire une fiche courte : texte, sens, thème, pratique, question de bilan, piège à éviter.
Ne pas imposer une seule intention quotidienne : autoriser plusieurs intentions par thème.
Ne pas obliger à écrire longuement : prévoir des choix rapides et une note optionnelle.
Distinguer matin, journée et soir : intention, rappel en situation, bilan/réparation.
Ajouter une page Iskandarī dédiée : œuvres, thèmes, sagesses, al-Tanwīr, Laṭāʾif, prières intimes, parcours.
